case.title: OREGON EX REL. SMITH v. GLADDEN

opinion.h1: OREGON EX REL. SMITH v. GLADDEN, 368 U.S. 8 (1961)
opinion.h4: OREGON EX REL. SMITH v. GLADDEN, WARDEN. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OREGON. No. 23, Misc. Decided October 9, 1961.
opinion.p: Robert Y. Thornton, Attorney General of Oregon, and Harold W. Adams, Assistant Attorney General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h4: HAMILTON v. SUPERIOR COURT OF CALIFORNIA, 368 U.S. 8 (1961)
opinion.p: 368 U.S. 8 HAMILTON, ALIAS HAYDEN, v. SUPERIOR COURT OF CALIFORNIA, IN AND FOR MARIN COUNTY. APPEAL FROM THE DISTRICT COURT OF APPEAL OF CALIFORNIA, FIRST APPELLATE
No. 105, Misc. Decided October 9, 1961. Appeal dismissed and certiorari denied.
Richard Gladstein and Norman Leonard for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
